bead.title: ui: toggle race during in-flight target-policy fetch on Links preview
bead.description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of c28a750.
File: ui/src/routes/tenants/[tenant]/databases/[database]/collections/[name]/+page.svelte:362-374 (toggleLinkPreview)
Category: correctness (UX)
Severity: medium
Description: toggleLinkPreview marks expanded state ONLY after `await ensureTargetPolicy(...)` resolves. If the user clicks the toggle button a second time while the policy fetch is still in flight, expandedLinks[key] is still falsy on entry, so the second click also takes the expand branch instead of toggling closed. Result: a rapid double-click leaves the row expanded when the user expected it to collapse, and may issue a redundant fetch (mitigated by the cache only after the first await completes).
Suggested fix: Either (a) set expandedLinks[key] = true optimistically BEFORE the await so subsequent clicks see the truthy value and the existing collapse branch handles them, or (b) track an explicit pendingExpand Set and ignore re-entrant toggle clicks while a fetch is in flight. (a) is preferable because it preserves user-initiated cancellation; pair it with a guard so a fetch failure rolls expandedLinks[key] back to false.
bead.acceptance: Rapidly clicking the inline target-data toggle on the same row twice leaves the row collapsed (or visibly mid-fetch) rather than expanded. Add a Playwright assertion: click toggle twice without awaiting; expect previewRow toBeHidden and the redacted-field count to be 0. policy-enforcement.spec.ts continues to pass.
